title: Slow Cooked Slovakian Ground Beef Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Slovakian dish featuring ground beef, perfect for a hangover cure, made keto-friendly and cooked to perfection in a Korean brass bowl using sous-vide techniques.
instructions: Brown the ground beef in a skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown.
Stir in the beef broth, tomato paste, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Transfer the mixture to a Korean brass bowl and cook in a sous-vide water bath at 130 degrees Fahrenheit for 8 hours.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and serve the stew hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Serve the stew in the Korean brass bowl, making sure to handle the hot bowl with care.
